name: Garlic Mud Chicken
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F (about 50 cloves of garlic, or as needed).
2. Blend peeled garlic cloves with olive oil and Cajun spices until creamy.
3. If necessary, add more oil to achieve the desired consistency.
4. Transfer the oil and garlic mixture into a large bowl.
5. Gradually add flour while mixing until a very heavy, thick, smooth mud-like consistency is achieved.
6. Tie chicken wings and legs tightly against the sides of the carcass using string.
7. Coat the entire chicken evenly with the Garlic Mud using a spatula, filling in all crevices between wings and legs.
8. Place the chicken on a rack, breast side up, in a pan and put it into the preheated oven.
9. Roast the whole chicken for 1½ to 2½ hours, or until the coating is well browned.
10. If using cut-up pieces of chicken, coat each piece thoroughly, place them on a rack, and roast for a shorter time until the coating is brown.
11. Serve the chicken with crisp garlic pieces.
This recipe is a modification of an old Chinese dish called Beggar's Chicken, which used mud to encase the chicken before cooking it in hot coals.
